title: Beijing pushes for China-Pak-style economic corridor with Bangladesh and Myanmar
content: China is pushing for an economic corridor linking Bangladesh and Myanmar, mirroring CPEC's strategic importance. This initiative, discussed during Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman's China visit, aims to boost connectivity, with China developing the Mongla port. Beijing has also expressed openness to other nations joining this "transnational initiative," which has drawn India's attention due to its proximity to the eastern border.

title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce secretly married in intimate ceremony: Report
content: Pop sensation Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce have reportedly exchanged vows in a private ceremony, according to a New York Post report.  Sources suggest the couple, both 36, are now legally married, with a grand celebration for over a thousand guests planned at Madison Square Garden later this week.  This intimate union precedes a larger event, hinting at a significant milestone for the high-profile pair.

title: India's trains face a fresh wave of threats — and the red flags are everywhere
content: India's railway network faces escalating security threats, from widespread stone-pelting incidents causing significant damage to premium trains, to track sabotage and even improvised explosives. These acts, driven by mischief, frustration, and criminal intent, are testing the rail security apparatus. Authorities are deploying advanced technology like drones and solar cameras, alongside community outreach, to combat these challenges and ensure passenger safety.

title: After Meta layoffs, Zuckerberg says firms should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: After WhatsApp, Centre sends notices to Telegram, Signal
content: Following a directive to Meta, the Indian government has now issued notices to Telegram and Signal, demanding explanations on their existing username systems. Authorities are seeking details on how these platforms prevent fraud, impersonation, and scams, giving them three days to respond. The government also questioned Telegram's continued offering of its username feature, citing concerns over potential cybercrime.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: PM Modi, Japan's Sanae Takaichi push for early Quad meet
content: Prime Minister Modi and his Japanese counterpart reaffirmed commitment to an early Quad summit, stressing freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz and opposing restrictive measures.  The joint statement unequivocally condemned cross-border terrorism from Pakistan, specifically mentioning groups like Jaish and Lashkar, and supported India's IEA membership. Both nations also voiced opposition to unilateral actions in the East and South China Seas.

title: Patriots, 49ers emerge as top trade fits for Raiders' $106.5m star
content: The Las Vegas Raiders’ star player Maxx Crosby has been a regular name concerning trade rumors ever since his trade to the Baltimore Ravens failed reportedly because of a physical test back in March 2026. Amid all the mess, it seems the New England Patriots and the San Francisco 49ers have emerged as potential landing spots for Maxx Crosby, ahead of the new season.

title: Omar Abdullah backs call for India-Pak talks, says even RSS favours dialogue
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah advocated for renewed India-Pakistan dialogue, emphasizing it's the sole path to de-escalate tensions. He questioned the criticism faced by J&K politicians for similar calls, contrasting it with the lack of backlash for RSS leaders advocating open talks. Abdullah highlighted the importance of improving neighborly relations, referencing former PM Vajpayee's sentiment.

title: Microsoft plans another round of layoffs, may cut thousands of jobs across teams
content: Microsoft is gearing up for another round of job cuts, reportedly impacting sales, consulting, and Xbox divisions. While smaller than previous reductions, thousands of positions may be affected. This move comes as the tech giant prioritizes AI investments and cost control, even as its stock has seen a dip.  A voluntary retirement program earlier this year also aimed to manage headcount.

title: To avoid legal battles, ED settles 150 Fema cases with RBI nod
content: The Enforcement Directorate is opting for large-scale compounding of Foreign Exchange Management Act (Fema) cases, with the Reserve Bank of India's approval, to expedite resolutions and avoid lengthy legal battles. Over 150 Fema cases have been terminated in the past 15 months following "no-objection certificates" from the ED. This approach, involving penalty payments, aims to foster a more facilitative business environment in India.

title: Bank of Baroda enters 5.7k crore settlement with creditors of NMC Health
content: Bank of Baroda has settled a major legal dispute, agreeing to pay $600 million to administrators of the collapsed NMC Health group. This out-of-court agreement concludes years of litigation concerning cross-border insolvency and alleged fraud following NMC's 2020 downfall. The payment will be distributed to creditors, with the bank's shares seeing a reaction to the news.

title: 'Minor' drop in mileage of vehicles due to E20, says Hardeep Singh Puri
content: Petroleum Minister Hardeep Singh Puri addressed public concerns regarding ethanol-blended fuel, dismissing social media narratives as "make-believe." He clarified that any mileage reduction in E20-fuelled vehicles is minor, with potential benefits like improved acceleration.  The ministry has released a detailed note to counter misinformation and reassure vehicle owners about the safety and established practice of ethanol blending, citing historical support from figures like Henry Ford.

title: NFL Trade Rumor: 49ers, Cowboys linked to Browns' $4.65M QB
content: The Cleveland Browns’ star player Shedeur Sanders has been a popular name concerning trade rumors ahead of the new season, sparking concern about his future. Amid all the teams that have been linked to him, it seems the Dallas Cowboys and the San Francisco 49ers have emerged as potential landing spots for him.

title: Alex Ovechkin's NHL future in doubt after final-game claim by insider
content: Alex Ovechkin has yet to confirm whether he will return for another NHL season, but recent developments suggest retirement is becoming increasingly likely. TSN insider Darren Dreger believes Washington's offseason moves indicate the Capitals expect their captain not to return. Ovechkin has said family, health and the team's Stanley Cup outlook will guide his decision, while head coach Spencer Carbery and general manager Chris Patrick maintain that no final choice has been made. As the organization waits for an answer, attention remains fixed on what could be the closing chapter of one of the NHL's greatest careers.

title: NHL Trade Rumor: Penguins, Rangers linked to $120M Stars scorer
content: Jason Robertson’s contract negotiations with the Dallas Stars are ongoing, and arbitration is a real possibility. Teams like the Pittsburgh Penguins and New York Rangers appear to be interested in the uncertainty, while Dallas is still looking for ways to keep one of the NHL’s top offensive talents before the 2026-27 season.

title: SC nixes rulings by NCLT, NCLAT based on fake AI citations
content: The Supreme Court has nullified orders from NCLT and NCLAT due to their reliance on fabricated AI-generated case laws. Justices cautioned against unchecked AI use, emphasizing human control in justice delivery. The court stressed a zero-tolerance approach for unverified AI precedents, declaring any decision based on such material invalid. A committee will now explore this issue, highlighting the risks of delegating critical thinking to AI.

title: Navy, Marcos secure crew after piracy bid on India-bound ship in Gulf of Aen
content: Indian Navy warship INS Trikand successfully thwarted a piracy attempt on an India-bound merchant vessel, MV Golden Arsenal, in the Gulf of Aden. Marine commandos boarded the ship, which carries an Indian sailor, and secured it after the crew took refuge in the citadel. The vessel sustained some damage. This incident highlights the Navy's ongoing commitment to maritime security in the region.

title: Mehbooba, J&K politicians fly to Iran for Khamenei funeral
content: Mehbooba Mufti, the sole non-Shia politician from J&K and Ladakh, flew to Tehran for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral, an event arranged with a special aircraft due to suspended commercial flights. Several other prominent figures from the region were also invited. The invitation highlighted the deep historical and strategic ties between Iran and India, underscoring the significance of the attendees' presence.

title: Defence ministry may clear proposals worth over Rs 1 lakh crore today
content: Defence Ministry gears up to approve over Rs 1 lakh crore in acquisitions, including 300 K-9 Vajra howitzers and 600 HAMMER missiles for fighter jets. The Army will also bolster its air defence with Russian Verba systems. This significant procurement push aims to modernize India's military capabilities, with key defence chiefs attending the crucial Defence Acquisition Council meeting for the first time.

title: Pharma pricing norms for new drugs eased
content: The government has significantly reformed the Drugs (Prices Control) Order, 2013, easing compliance for drugmakers. Overcharging liability is now narrowed, focusing only on stock sold above ceiling prices by retailers or distributors, not entire batches.  New drug launches will also see simplified pricing approvals, reducing procedural delays and streamlining market entry for similar products.

title: NHAI sacks Delhi-Mumbai expressway contractor after viral road damage video
content: A viral video showing a damaged section of the Delhi-Mumbai Expressway in Rajasthan has sparked renewed criticism. The National Highways Authority of India terminated the original contractor's agreement in April due to persistent poor performance.  Repairs are underway by a new builder at the former contractor's expense, with micro-milling and permanent reconstruction planned after traffic diversion. Experts are investigating the cause of the pavement distress.

title: Rajasthan Royals get major relief as HC removes key hurdle to IPL in Jaipur
content: Rajasthan Cricket Association's ad hoc committee has been dissolved by the High Court, paving the way for democratic elections within three months. A sole administrator has been appointed to oversee this process. This judicial intervention is expected to revive stalled projects like the Chonp stadium and secure the Rajasthan Royals' IPL home matches in Jaipur, addressing long-standing administrative and financial disputes.

title: 'Informal' MHA meeting on Ladakh today
content: Informal discussions are set to resume in Leh between central officials and Ladakh groups, focusing on previous meeting outcomes and other pressing matters. This follows a recent shutdown protesting alleged backtracking on key agreements, including a legislative body and constitutional safeguards. While a prominent activist continues a hunger strike in Delhi, these talks aim to bridge the gap on crucial demands for the Union Territory's future.

title: Amarnath Yatra 2026: J&K Lt Guv flags off 1st batch of pilgrims from Jammu
content: Jammu and Kashmir Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha initiated the 57-day Amarnath Yatra 2026 from Jammu, flagging off over 4,800 pilgrims.  Extensive security, including armoured vehicles and aerial surveillance, is in place for the journey to the holy cave shrine.  The pilgrimage, commencing July 3 and concluding August 28, aims to provide a safe and spiritually fulfilling experience for devotees.

title: Chipflation is here and you'll have to pay for it
content: The AI boom is driving up the cost of everyday electronics, a phenomenon dubbed 'chipflation'.  Memory chip prices have surged dramatically, impacting everything from smart TVs and smartphones to kitchen appliances.  Manufacturers are diverting chip production to AI infrastructure, leading to scarcity and price hikes for consumers.  This could significantly slow down upgrades for budget-conscious buyers, with global smartphone shipments expected to hit a decade low.

title: 17-year-old strangles wife over 'repeated taunts', dumps body with father's help
content: A 17-year-old boy has been detained for allegedly murdering his 20-year-old wife in Prayagraj. He reportedly confessed to strangling her on June 11 after she taunted him. His father allegedly helped dispose of the body in Fatehpur. The woman's father had filed a missing person report after failing to contact his daughter for two days. Police are searching for the parents.

title: Fyodor Dostoevsky’s quote on pain & suffering: Timeless lesson on resilience
content: Fyodor Dostoevsky, a literary giant, delved into the human psyche, exploring guilt, faith, and redemption. His profound insights, shaped by personal suffering including a near-death execution and Siberian imprisonment, influenced psychology and philosophy. Dostoevsky's novels, like 'Crime and Punishment' and 'The Karamazov Brothers,' reveal that deep intelligence and compassion often come with unavoidable sadness, a truth that continues to resonate today.

title: Wild fan theory brands Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift 'the same person'
content: A viral X post has sparked a fan theory suggesting Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are the same person. The NFL power couple is dubbed The Siamese Twins. The user compiled a video showcasing their remarkably similar gestures from clapping and waving to dancing and posing for photos. While the theory is playful, experts note that couples often adopt each other's habits over time due to shared experiences and a natural inclination towards similarity. They truly complete one another.

title: 'Only those who deliver will travel': Sports min on Asian Games selection policy
content: With 2026 Asian Games qualification process nearing its end, Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya made clear that only those teams and athletes who can bring laurels to the country will be green lit. The extensive personal support staff will not be approved at the public's expense.

title: 'Enough is enough': Ex-England captain demands McCullum resign after Stokes retirement
content: Michael Vaughan has launched a fierce critique of England's cricket leadership, demanding the resignations of coach Brendon McCullum and managing director Rob Key following Ben Stokes' international retirement.  Vaughan expressed deep dissatisfaction with the team's dismal recent Test record, stating English cricket must strive for better than its current seventh-place ranking. He believes sweeping changes are necessary to revive the team's fortunes and restore pride.

title: 17-year-old girl electrocuted near apartment gate in Thane; power officials booked
content: Tragedy struck Thane as a 17-year-old girl died from electric shock in a waterlogged area outside her Mumbra home. Her father alleges Torrent Power ignored prior complaints about voltage issues. Separately, a Bhiwandi sweet shop worker was electrocuted by a live wire near a temple, sparking protests over alleged negligence and attempts to hide the faulty wiring. Investigations are underway for both incidents.

title: Gauahar defends Zaid Darbar: Slams Nikhil Chinapa over alliance ‘gameplay’; viewers back Zaid
content: Gauahar Khan passionately defended Zaid Darbar when confronting Nikhil Chinapa's critique of the game. She accused Chinapa of misinterpreting the events and being politically motivated, while praising Zaid for his honesty and commitment to resolving conflicts openly. Khan challenged Chinapa's own credibility, raising concerns about his inconsistent judgments and sparking lively discussion among viewers regarding Zaid's transparent approach in the game.

title: Telegram, Signal under Centre's scanner over username feature after WhatsApp
content: Indian authorities are ramping up their scrutiny of messaging apps, questioning both Telegram and Signal about their unique username features. This move mirrors previous discussions with WhatsApp concerning alarming trends in online fraud and impersonation. The government is keen on understanding how these platforms ensure user safety and traceability, prompting Telegram to clarify its current functionalities regarding username use.

title: Which faction is 'real' TMC? EC seeks claims, counterclaims from rival camps by July 6
content: The Election Commission has asked rival Trinamool Congress factions to submit claims regarding party signatories and organizational elections by July 6. This follows a meeting where rebel leader Ritabrata Banerjee presented his group's case, asserting they represent the "real" TMC with significant MLA support. The Mamata Banerjee camp, however, questioned the EC's decision to meet the rebels, alleging procedural violations and political influence.

title: 'What's our tolerance?': Aus ready to risk Ellyse Perry's fitness for T20 WC final
content: Perry retired hurt during Australia's semi-final win over West Indies at The Oval after feeling what the team described as "minor quad awareness". She walked off after facing seven balls, with Australia later saying the decision was taken as a precaution because they were in control of the match.

title: US pilot 'killed', plane torched in Papua; rebels call attack a 'message' to America & Indonesia
content: Separatist rebels in Papua claim to have killed an American pilot and burned a civilian plane, alleging it ferried Indonesian military personnel. The West Papua National Liberation Army stated the attack was a message to the US and Indonesian governments. Authorities confirmed the aircraft was found burnt but could not immediately verify the rebels' claims or the pilot's death. This incident escalates the ongoing low-level insurgency in the region.

title: No doctor's note, no sick leave? Germany plans major workplace shake-up
content: Under Chancellor Friedrich Merz, the government has rolled out a transformative economic reform initiative intended to enhance Germany's financial landscape. The plan encompasses significant tax cuts for families, adjustments in the pension system that raise the retirement age, and new sick leave policies that require doctor’s notes from day one.

title: New EPF Scheme 2026: What it means for your provident fund savings
content: India’s provident fund framework has now entered a new phase with the notification of the Employees’ Provident Funds Scheme, 2026 (EPF Scheme, 2026) under the Code on Social Security, 2020.

title: Kuki village torched in Manipur’s Noney district in pre-dawn attack
content: This follows similar attacks on Kuki and Naga villages in Kamjong district, near the India-Myanmar border, on July 1

title: Boulder collapse near Bengaluru: Half-eaten meals remain where a routine workday at quarry turned fatal
content: Most of the workers employed at the quarry are migrant labourers from Yadgir and Raichur in Karnataka, and from Jharkhand, Bihar and Madhya Pradesh

title: VB-G RAM G scheme rolled out from Mukkavaripalli village in Andhra Pradesh
content: Launched at Mukkavaripalli in Tirupati district, the renewed rural employment scheme puts farmer welfare first; the Centre also offered to procure 2.16 lakh tonnes of Totapuri mangoes

title: A shot at life, Mandsaur’s model for HPV vaccination
content: This district in Madhya Pradesh has demonstrated effective grassroots strategies for HPV vaccination delivery

title: J&K CM Omar Abdullah backs efforts to resume dialogue with Pakistan
content: Refers to remarks by Vajpayee, and the RSS chief while calling for improved relations with the neighbouring country

title: Wangchuk's health worsens; CJP alleges police assault
content: Dipke reiterates demand for Pradhan's resignation; agitation receives fresh backing from CPI(ML) Liberation General Secretary Dipankar Bhattacharya and RTI activist Nikhil Dey

title: India remains on top in AIU's June list of global dope offenders
content: India has ranked in the top two for the most Anti-Doping Rule Violations (ADRVs) in athletics between 2022 and 2025

title: Transfer of narcotics convict from Sri Lanka to India: Madras High Court penalises MEA & MHA officials
content: Justices Anita Sumanth and Sunder Mohan direct the two joint secretaries in the ministries to pay ₹50,000 to High Court Legal Services Committee

title: SC refuses urgent hearing on plea against Madras HC order quashing appointment of personal assistants to judges
content: The High Court had found several ‘illegalities and irregularities’ in the appointment process undertaken in 2023

title: Privatisation of power distribution: Escoms to submit objections before KERC
content: Escoms have been asked to submit their disagreement and inform the KERC that the government does not concur with the application: CM

title: Karnataka set for rollout of VB-G RAM G scheme
content: Legal decks have been cleared for the rollout of the VB-G RAM G scheme in Karnataka with the State government issuing a notification in this regard on Thursday. The notification comes on the eve of the meeting of RDPR Ministers convened by the Centre in Delhi

title: Two Kashmir Shia clerics ‘disallowed’ to travel to Iran for Ali Khamenei’s final rites
content: J&K Anjuman-e-Sharie Shian president Aga Syed Hassan Al-Mosavi Al-Safavi was stopped by Bureau of Immigration; Shia cleric Masroor Abbas Ansari was also “denied a passport”

title: Debilitating disability: struggles of families of children with chronic disabilities in Kerala
content: Families of children with chronic diseases are struggling to manage their lives and the medical treatment of their wards, finds C. Maya. Experts in the field highlight the need to evolve a societal support system for the families

title: ‘Agri’ Krishnamurthy challenges deserters to prove the alleged bid to form government with DMK’s support
content: All the 47 legislators who had won on the ticket of the AIADMK had adopted a resolution, electing Palaniswami the leader of the legislature party, he says

title: Cauvery realisation by Tamil Nadu in June back to the usual deficit pattern
content: the State realised a little less than three thousand million cubic feet, as against the stipulated 9.19 tmc ft for the month

title: Indian team discovers rare ‘bow and arrow’ radio galaxy two billion light years from Earth
content: The RAD-BAARG may be one of the clearest known radio signatures of a giant bow shock generated by a galaxy falling supersonically into a cluster environment, the researchers say

title: ‘Jana Nayagan’ likely to get CBFC nod after long delay, official says
content: The film’s release, originally slated for January, may finally be released at some point following the issue of a certificate in the coming days

title: Assam labourer shows 15 documents but fails citizenship test in High Court
content: The documents Aminul Hoque, a daily-wage labourer, submitted included a 1973 land purchase deed, a school certificate, and a copy of the 1951 National Register of Citizens

title: After WhatsApp, Telegram and Signal receive notices on username feature
content: The notices follow directions to WhatsApp to pause its username feature over scam and impersonation concerns; Spokespersons for Telegram, Signal did not have an immediate comment to share

title: Krishnalanka police station staff destroyed evidences in custodial death case: SIT
content: Accused constables did not answer any queries during the questioning, SIT officials tell court; head constable Sambaiah and constable Babu Rao allegedly deleted CCTV footage and other evidence

title: Chennai Corporation fixes September 30 as deadline for road cuts by other service departments
content: The civic body requires all departments to conclude excavations by this date. It will authorise subsequent road cuts only after December. Work to restore major road cuts is already under way

title: Will bring out policy to make getting education loans easier, says P. Viswanathan
content: He says all Collectors have been instructed to organise counselling sessions for students with bank managers in their respective districts to help them acquire loans to pursue higher education

title: No word on hike in minimum pension, allege trade unions
content: The notification does not reflect the demands of subscribers such as increase in minimum pension and clarity in providing higher pension for the applicants, trade unions said

title: Centre amends Drugs Rules, 1945 to ensure uniform regulation of advance cell and gene therapies
content: Cell or stem cell-derived products such as stem cell-based regenerative treatments and CAR-T cell therapies have seen increasing use in treatment of blood cancers such as leukemias and lymphomas; the Centre’s move is aimed at strengthening regulatory oversight of such advanced and emerging medical technologies

title: Free and rules-based Indo-Pacific a shared priority for India and Japan: PM Modi
content: Japanese counterpart Sanae Takaichi says India and Japan are ‘perfectly aligned’ and emphasised on the need for ‘strategic cooperation’; both leaders attend economic forum

title: BJP urges State govt. to hand over to CBI misappropriation of funds case
content: The case relates to misappropriation of multi-crore funds in Matoshri Hemareddy Mallamma Souharda Sahakara Sangha

title: Ayodhya lawyers hold protest march, demand FIR against Champat Rai and others
content: Dozens of lawyers stormed into Station House Officer’s office in Ayodhya urging the immediate filing of a case, while police officials attempted to pacify them by assuring that appropriate legal action would follow

title: Indian Railways registers growth in freight, passenger services
content: The Railways loaded 142.21 million tonnes of freight during June 2026, compared with 136.71 million tonnes in June 2025; freight earnings generated an additional revenue of about ₹430 crore

title: Maharashtra government stays RTI Rules two days before Anna Hazare’s agitation
content: RTI activists demand public consultation, scrapping of the amendments, and transparency before taking further decision

title: India should not chase costly LLM race; instead, it should focus on practical AI models: Mohandas Pai
content: He was speaking at the launch of fintech firm ToneTag’s voice-first merchant banking platform eKosha here on Wednesday.

title: West Bengal Cabinet approves panel to examine draft Uniform Civil Code Bill
content: Minister Agnimitra Paul says the committee headed by retired Supreme Court judge Ranjana Prakash Desai will have four weeks to scrutinise the draft legislation and submit its recommendations

title: First batch of Amarnath pilgrims flagged off from Jammu
content: The first batch of more than 4,800 pilgrims has set off on the holy yatra in a convoy of 259 light and heavy vehicles, officials say; security on the yatra routes has been stepped up

title: Madhya Pradesh judge faces mob fury as suo motu case to protect judicial officers sits mute in Supreme Court
content: Supreme Court Advocates on Record Association slams recent threats, targeted social media campaign against MP judge; 2021 case to safeguard judges, courts was last posted for hearing in March 2025

title: Accused cannot claim default bail for non-filing of charge sheet copies: Supreme Court judgment
content: Default bail could be claimed only if the charge sheet itself was not filed within the statutory 60 or 90 days of investigation, the judgment notes

title: Labour Ministry makes EPF contributions beyond ₹1,800 voluntary
content: Under the old scheme, the Employees' Provident Funds Scheme 1952, the wage ceiling, which is ₹15,000 per month at present, was relevant at the time of joining a firm for providing the employee with mandatory social security cover.

title: Wish I had an antidote to insecurities, says Manish Tewari after Punjab Congress revamp
content: Mr. Tewari, the Chandigarh MP, shared on X The Hindu’s report on the Congress’s Punjab revamp, which said he had been ‘left in the cold’ as the party announced new working presidents and election panels ahead of 2027 Assembly polls

title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce Donate $26 Million To Charities Ahead Of Wedding
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have donated $26 million to charities this week ahead of their Friday wedding at Madison Square Garden.

title: Mark Zuckerberg Says AI Agent Development Going Slower Than Expected
content: Meta Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg told an internal town hall on Thursday that AI agent development over the last four months has not "accelerated in the way we expected," according to a recording heard by Reuters.

title: Turkish Comedian Arrested For Allegedly Insulting Islam
content: A popular Turkish comedian was arrested on arrival at Istanbul airport on Thursday over a stand-up show that involved material that prosecutors said was allegedly offensive to Islam.

title: "Ill-Advised": Trump's Massive July 4 Firework Show Raises Health Alarms
content: That's the question as Washington prepares to break the record for the world's largest fireworks display on the United States' 250th birthday on Saturday.

title: No MBBS Degree For Student Till Fees Traced To Maoist Group Repaid: Court
content: A Division Bench held that while educational certificates generally cannot be withheld over fee disputes, this case stood on a completely different footing because the fee paid had been seized by the National Investigation Agency as alleged proceeds of crime.

title: Russia Launches Intense Drone And Missile Attack On Kyiv Killing 21
content: Loud explosions shook the Ukrainian capital, where more than 50,000 people sheltered in subway stations after authorities issued air raid warnings, the Kyiv Metro said.

title: Army Chief Dhiraj Seth Calls On Defence Minister Rajnath Singh
content: Gen Dhiraj Seth, who took charge as the new Army Chief on June 30, called on Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, officials said on Thursday.

title: 9 Killed In Cafe Blast In Syria's Damascus
content: The Interior Ministry is set to announce its initial findings soon, said Damascus Governor Maher Idlibi.

title: "They Are Using Ethanol In Racing Cars": Oil Minister Hardeep Puri
content: Puri defended the government's push towards greater biofuel blending while addressing the heavy financial toll on oil marketing companies amid recent global crude price volatility triggered by the Middle East crisis.

title: Names Of 10 Founding Nations For Global Defence Bank Soon: Canada
content: The bank's purpose is to bolster the defence of allied nations by raising up to £100 billion ($133 billion) in cheap finance.

title: Buried In Rubble After Earthquake For 8 Days, Venezuela Man Rescued Alive
content: Hernan Alberto Gil Flores emerged to safety covered in dust atop a stretcher surrounded by helmet-clad rescue workers after being trapped since June 24 under rubble in the basement of the Galerias Playa Grande shopping center in the coastal town in La Guaira.

title: 3 Chinese Nationals Arrested At Delhi Airport For Carrying Rs 19 Lakh Undeclared Cash
content: The passengers, bound for Shanghai, were handed over to Customs authorities for further investigation.

title: 'Will Surrender Before Court': Jailed Gangster On Salman Khan House Firing
content: Anmol, the younger brother of jailed gangster Lawrence Bishnoi, was deported from the US in November last year and was subsequently arrested by the National Investigation Agency.

title: India-Japan Summit Expands Strategic Partnership Across Defence And Trade
content: The summit marked Takaichi's first official visit to India after assuming office and came at a time of growing geopolitical uncertainty, supply chain disruptions and heightened security challenges across the Indo Pacific.

title: Ghaziabad Boy, 17, Dies After Assault Over Road Rage Incident, 2 Arrested
content: The victim was returning home with his friends on a motorcycle after visiting a swimming pool on the Behta Hajipur canal road on Monday when the two-wheeler collided with a car near the Indraprastha Gas Plant crossing.

title: PM Modi And Takaichi Deepen India-Japan Ties On AI And Technology
content: The joint statement, issued in the capital, frames AI as a defining technology whose governance choices today will shape economic security and international power balances for decades.

title: Criminal Attacks Senior Gujarat Cop With Knife, Gets Shot In Leg
content: The senior police officer drew his service weapon and fired a shot into the suspect's leg. He sustained a minor injury in the knife attack.

title: "We'll Die Inside": 3 Deaths Reveal Depth Of Delhi's Manual Scavenging Crisis
content: Three people, the factory owner Suraj Marwah and contractors Jayant and Neeraj have been arrested. A case has been filed under the Manual Scavenging Act and the SC/ST Act.

title: Trump Posts AI Video Of Him As Doctor, Mocks Celebrities Like Julia Roberts
content: The clip was posted on Trump's social media platform, Truth Social. It later appeared on X after White House Deputy Chief of Staff Dan Scavino Jr shared it.

title: Delhi-Doon expressway: NHAI cracks down on project officials

title: When chemistry beats caste: A quiet revolt is rewriting the oldest rule of arranged marriage
content: defining questions today are no longer limited to surname, caste or community . they are about emotional maturity, career ambitions, financial compatibility, family expectations, mental health, parenting, equality and shared life goals . compatibility is steadily replacing community as the most valuable currency .

title: Trump's big bang theory: US capital ready for biggest pyrotechnics in history -- in stifling heat
content: temperatures in much of the US are forecast to hit record highs on July 4 . more than 860,000 pyrotechnic effects will light up the skies during a 40-minute finale . this year, "spectacular" is being bumped up to "supersized"
